model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds the edge as the #1 overall seed with elite metrics on both ends of the floor (KenPom/Torvik rank #2, offense rank 7-8, defense rank 2), while Iowa State's strong defensive profile (#4 ranked defense, DRtg 95.6) is offset by a weaker offense (ORtg 120.0, 20th nationally). The Wolverines' balanced attack and superior overall efficiency should exploit Iowa State's offensive limitations, though the Cyclones' defensive intensity and Big 12 experience under T.J. Otzelberger make this a competitive matchup. Michigan's minor injury concerns with Yaxel Lendeborg's ankle are a factor, but their overall talent depth and tournament pedigree give them the advantage in this Elite 8 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Elite 8 Midwest Regional Final: Michigan vs Iowa State
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan's elite two-way profile gives them a slight edge over Iowa State's defensive prowess in a tightly contested matchup.
evidence: Michigan: 31-2 record, KenPom/Torvik rank #2, offense rank 7-8, defense rank 2, SRS 38.38
Iowa State: 27-7 record, KenPom rank #6, DRtg 95.6 (6th), ORtg 120.0 (20th), SRS 27.12
Michigan's superior overall efficiency (adj_em 38.38 vs Iowa State's 27.12 SRS) indicates a significant talent gap
Iowa State's defensive strength (#4 ranked defense) could limit Michigan's offense, but their weaker offensive efficiency (20th) may struggle against Michigan's elite defense (rank 2)
Both teams have tournament experience, but Michigan's #1 overall seed status and balanced profile provide a psychological and analytical edge

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.
